page: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The CPU includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and a 36 MB L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable strength, ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, are relatively weak. Memory bandwidth is also mixed, showing average performance for large-block read operations but weak performance for small-block reads and large-block writes.

The instance exhibits poor performance in Passmark CPU encryption and overall Passmark memory metrics, alongside weak results in software compilation, image processing, and Redis SET operations. For lightweight workloads, it delivers average performance in database operations and text processing. Given its hardware profile, the c8g.large is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level compute-focused tasks that do not require high memory density or local storage, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and single-threaded utility scripts.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, 36 MB of L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, operating under the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded decompression capabilities, though multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic database operations, text processing, and entry-level web serving workloads where a low-resource, dedicated ARM processor is qualitatively cost-effective.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores for lightweight single-threaded processing and basic database workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory with a 36 MB L3 cache configuration
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression and average text processing performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included in the configuration
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and single-threaded tasks
